summary: Iteration 19 customer_type feature fully validated. Backend pytest 8/8 passed; Frontend UI integration + E2E (Just Inquiry → Follow-up; Instant Buyer → Inquiry with Aadhaar upload) all green.

backend_tests: GET /api/constants → contains customer_types = [Instant Buyer, Token Finance Buyer, Just Inquiry] (PASS)
POST /api/leads customer_type='Token Finance Buyer' → stage='Inquiry', persisted (PASS)
frontend_tests: Login as superadmin — successful redirect to /dashboard
Lead form renders [data-testid='customer-type-grid'] with 3 buttons (instant-buyer, token-finance-buyer, just-inquiry)
Selecting any button applies brand color highlight (border-brand bg-brand/5)
Documents (Identity KYC / staged-aadhaar) HIDDEN when no selection
Documents VISIBLE when 'Instant Buyer' selected (staged-aadhaar slot present)
Documents VISIBLE when 'Token Finance Buyer' selected
Toggling back to 'Just Inquiry' hides Documents again
E2E: Create lead via UI as 'Just Inquiry' → LeadDetail loads at Follow-up stage (FOLLOW-UP badge active, 'Next: Hold' button, Inquiry stage skipped)
E2E: Create lead via UI as 'Instant Buyer' with staged Aadhaar JPEG → lead created at Inquiry stage, identity_docs.aadhaar populated (count=1), file visible under 'IDENTITY (AADHAAR)' on detail page

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: backend strips whitespace then validates whitelist — good. customer_type stored as None when empty/missing — correct.
Minor: Frontend wantsDocs gate (LeadForm.jsx:146) correctly skips identity uploads for Just Inquiry, preventing wasted API calls.
Suggest (non-blocking): /api/leads PUT also accepts customer_type updates — may want to validate whitelist on update too (currently only validated on create). Not in scope of this iteration's tests.
seed_data_creation: None — created and cleaned up TEST_Iter19* leads only. Production mode preserved.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 19 (customer_type) is fully validated end-to-end. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iter19_customer_type.py is reusable for regression. Note: pytest must be run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported in env (not auto-loaded from .env). Frontend cleanup via fetch with localStorage token works fine.
